page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9a.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio without hyperthreading. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance for workloads requiring stable compute resources.

The hardware profile includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. This resource density aligns with standard general-purpose configurations. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and dedicated core allocation, the ecs.g9a.2xlarge is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application servers, microservices, and moderate database hosting. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for high-performance storage-heavy applications or parallelized machine learning tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9a.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.7 GHz, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with a single thread per core. The instance features 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This configuration offers a stable, dedicated compute environment suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and microservices that require consistent CPU performance without the need for local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
